DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THAT DEFENDANT UNFAIRLY INTERFERED WITH PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO RECEIVE THE BENEFITS OF THE ST. FRANCIS CIRCLE CONTRACT. THE PARTIES AGREED THAT PHOENIX ELECTRIC COMPANY WILL NOT AMEND THE CROSS COMPLAINT AND THE CAUSE OF ACTION AT ISSUE WILL BE DISMISSED. (302/LMG) JUDGE: LORETTA M. DISCOVERY, PLAINTIFF TITAN SERVICES, INC. APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER/ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT/TEMPORARY PROTECTIVE ORDER. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED - PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH AND TO ISSUE A WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $258,521.75 (AMOUNT OWED + ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS). UNDISPUTED FACTS ASSERTED BY PLAINTIFF SHOW THAT A SUBCONTRACT WAS EXECUTED BETWEEN THE PARTIES AND THE DEFENDANT FAILED TO PAY. FURTHER, WHILE DEFENDANT CLAIMS THAT THERE SHOULD BE AN OFFSET, DEFENDANT OFFERS NO EVIDENCE SUPPORTING THIS CLAIM. FINALLY, DEFENDANT FAILS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE, OR EVEN CLAIMS THAT IT WOULD BE PERSONALLY HARMED BY ISSUANCE OF A WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IN THIS CASE. DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENTS REGARDING IMPROPER SERVICE ARE UNPERSUASIVE. IN ADDITION, THE COURT RULED: PLAINTIFF HAS MET ITS EVIDENTIARY BURDEN TO SHOW IT WILL LIKELY PREVAIL. WHILE DEFENDANT CLAIMS THERE SHOULD BE AN OFFSET, DEFENDANT PROFFERS NO EVIDENCE SUPPORTING SUCH AN OFFSET CLAIMS AND THE FACT REMAINS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT BEEN PAID FOR WORK IT COMPLETED TO DEFENDANT'S SATISFACTION IN 2009. FURTHER DEFENDANT'S ORAL ARGUMENT OF HOW IT WOULD BE HARMED WAS SPECULATIVE AND NOT SUPPORTED BY EVIDENCE. DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT REGARDING DEFECTIVE SERVICE FAILS. THIS ISSUE DID NOT PREVENT DEFENDANT FROM HAVING AN OPPORTUNITY TO FULLY BRIEF THE COURT AND OPPOSE THE MOTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT TITAN SERVICES INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION OF PHOENIX ELECTRIC COMPANY'S CROSS-COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16 - PLAINTIFF/CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE APPLIES TO THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. A.F. BROWN ELEC. CONTRACTOR, INC. V. RHINO ELEC. SUPPLY, INC., (2006) 137 CAL.APP.4TH 1118, 1128. CCP 425.16 APPLIES TO A SUIT COMPLAINING ABOUT A STOP NOTICE WHERE SUCH A FILING IS DONE WHEN A LAWSUIT IS CONTEMPLATED IN GOOD FAITH AND GIVEN SERIOUS CONSIDERATION. TITAN'S SUPPORTING DECLARATIONS MEET THAT TEST. SECOND, PARAGRAPH 38 OF THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION COMPLAINS THAT TITAN BREACHED THE SUBCONTRACT BY FILING A CAUSE OF ACTION TO RECOVER ON THE SURETY BOND. THAT COMPONENT CLEARLY IMPLICATES CCP 425.16. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ARGUMENT THAT THE CLAIMS ARISE FROM THE SAME TRANSACTION IS TENUOUS. THE UNDERLYING CLAIM PERTAINS TO THE ELECTRICAL SERVICES AND WHETHER PAYMENT SHOULD BE MADE. THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION RELATES TO CROSS-DEFENDANT'S PROSECUTION OF A CLAIM IN COURT. FURTHERMORE, CROSS-COMPLAINANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE HOW IT WILL PREVAIL ON THE MERITS BECAUSE IT HAS NOT SHOWN HOW FILING A STOP NOTICE AND FILING A CAUSE OF ACTION TO RECOVER ON A BOND VIOLATES THE SUBCONTRACT. FINALLY THE COURT AWARDS ATTORNEY'S FEES TO PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT, HOWEVER WILL TAKE THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION AS TO THE AMOUNT. PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER WITH A BLANK LINE FOR THE AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES FOR THE COURT TO DETERMINE. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS AS TO THE REMAINING ISSUE OF ATTORNEY'S FEES: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS TO THE ATTORNEY'S FEES AWARD IN THE AMOUNT OF $5,541.50. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT TITAN SERVICES INC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT (of Phoenix Electric Company).
Appearances: Andrea Petray of Marks Golia & Finch (858.737.3100) appearing via Court Call for cross defendant and moving party; Fredrick J. Northrop of Last & Faoro (650.696.8360) appearing via Court Call for cross complainant and responding party.
Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts showing that defendant unfairly interfered with plaintiff's right to receive the benefits of the St. Francis circle contract. The parties agreed that Phoenix Electric Company will not amend the cross complaint and the cause of action at issue will be dismissed. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT TITAN SERVICES INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION OF PHOENIX ELECTRIC COMPANY'S CROSS-COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16.

APPEARANCES: Andrea L. Petray, Esq. of Marks, Golia & Finch, LLP 858/737-3100 for pltf/x-deft & moving party (appearing via CourtCall); Fredrick J. Northrop, Esq. of Last & Faoro 650/696-8350 for opposing party Phoenix Electric Company (appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as to the following: PLAINTIFF/CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE APPLIES TO THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. A.F. BROWN ELEC. CONTRACTOR, INC. V. RHINO ELEC. SUPPLY, INC., (2006) 137 CAL.APP.4TH 1118, 1128. CCP 425.16 APPLIES TO A SUIT COMPLAINING ABOUT A STOP NOTICE WHERE SUCH A FILING IS DONE WHEN A LAWSUIT IS CONTEMPLATED IN GOOD FAITH AND GIVEN SERIOUS CONSIDERATION. TITAN'S SUPPORTING DECLARATIONS MEET THAT TEST. SECOND, PARAGRAPH 38 OF THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION COMPLAINS THAT TITAN BREACHED THE SUBCONTRACT BY FILING A CAUSE OF ACTION TO RECOVER ON THE SURETY BOND. THAT COMPONENT CLEARLY IMPLICATES CCP 425.16. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ARGUMENT THAT THE CLAIMS ARISE FROM THE SAME TRANSACTION IS TENUOUS. THE UNDERLYING CLAIM PERTAINS TO THE ELECTRICAL SERVICES AND WHETHER PAYMENT SHOULD BE MADE. THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION RELATES TO CROSS-DEFENDANT'S PROSECUTION OF A CLAIM IN COURT. FURTHERMORE, CROSS-COMPLAINANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE HOW IT WILL PREVAIL ON THE MERITS BECAUSE IT HAS NOT SHOWN HOW FILING A STOP NOTICE AND FILING A CAUSE OF ACTION TO RECOVER ON A BOND VIOLATES THE SUBCONTRACT.
FINALLY THE COURT AWARDS ATTORNEY'S FEES TO PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT, however will take the matter under submission as to the amount.
Plaintiff/cross-defendant to submit a proposed form of order with a blank line for the award of attorney's fees for the Court to determine.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows as to the remaining issue of attorney's fees: Tentative ruling adopted as to the attorney's fees award in the amount of $5,541.50. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Notice And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Apr-15-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF TITAN SERVICES, INC. APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER/ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT/TEMPORARY PROTECTIVE ORDER.

APPEARANCES: Andrea L. Petray, Esq. of Marks, Golia & Finch, LLP 858/737-3100 for pltf & moving party (appearing via CourtCall)Frederick J. Northrop, Esq. of Last & Faoro 650/696-8350 for deft & opposing party Phoenix Electric Company; Thomas Dubberle, Esq. of McInerney & Dillon, P.C. 510/465-7100 for deft NTK Construction, Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted - PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH AND TO ISSUE A WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $258,521.75 (AMOUNT OWED + ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS). UNDISPUTED FACTS ASSERTED BY PLAINTIFF SHOW THAT A SUBCONTRACT WAS EXECUTED BETWEEN THE PARTIES AND THE DEFENDANT FAILED TO PAY. FURTHER, WHILE DEFENDANT CLAIMS THAT THERE SHOULD BE AN OFFSET, DEFENDANT OFFERS NO EVIDENCE SUPPORTING THIS CLAIM. FINALLY, DEFENDANT FAILS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE, OR EVEN CLAIMS THAT IT WOULD BE PERSONALLY HARMED BY ISSUANCE OF A WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IN THIS CASE. DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENTS REGARDING IMPROPER SERVICE ARE UNPERSUASIVE.
In addition, the Court ruled: Plaintiff has met its evidentiary burden to show it will likely prevail. While defendant claims there should be an offset, defendant proffers no evidence supporting such an offset claims and the fact remains that plaintiff has not been paid for work it completed to defendant's satisfaction in 2009. Further defendant's oral argument of how it would be harmed was speculative and not supported by evidence. Defendant's argument regarding defective service fails. This issue did not prevent defendant from having an opportunity to fully brief the Court and oppose the motion.
PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported
